Long-term exposure to the toxins in Senecio fraudulentus can also cause scarring and fibrosis in the liver. The symptoms of poisoning may include nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Senecio, a genus of flowering plants, is a type of daisy plant commonly known as ragwort or groundsel. The health benefits of Senecio species are attributed to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing pain and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Senecio species include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to their medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Senecio species include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, such as digestive issues and respiratory problems.
